name: Prostate Cancer - Androgen Deprivation Therapy Query
description: Tests ability to find prostate cancer patients via androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) and anti-androgens. ADT is highly prostate-specific: leuprolide (GnRH agonist), bicalutamide (anti-androgen), enzalutamide (next-gen anti-androgen), abiraterone (CYP17 inhibitor), degarelix (GnRH antagonist). All five drugs are essentially male-PCa-only.
prompt: Find patients prescribed prostate cancer hormonal therapy including leuprolide, bicalutamide, enzalutamide, abiraterone, or degarelix.

prompts.naive: Find patients on prostate cancer drugs.
prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed androgen deprivation therapy or anti-androgens for prostate cancer — GnRH agonists (leuprolide), GnRH antagonists (degarelix), first-generation anti-androgens (bicalutamide), and next-generation anti-androgens (enzalutamide, abiraterone).
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any prostate cancer hormonal therapy. Use these RxNorm ingredient codes: 42375 (leuprolide, GnRH agonist), 83008 (bicalutamide, anti-androgen), 1307298 (enzalutamide), 1100072 (abiraterone), 475230 (degarelix, GnRH antagonist). Combine in a single comma-separated code search. These drugs are essentially PCa-specific — minimal cross-indication.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with ADT/anti-androgen orders. High prostate-specificity drug class.
